description_long: This is a military-style attack airplane with a distinctive twin-engine configuration mounted above the fuselage and straight, low-mounted wings. The fuselage is painted in a light beige or off-white color with red accents on the wingtips, tail, and engine nacelles, and features various military insignia and nose art. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a dark, tinted canopy. The airplane has a tricycle landing gear arrangement, with two main wheels under the wings and a single nose wheel. The tail section features twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration. Visible details include a prominent nose cannon, various access panels, and surface markings typical of a ground-attack aircraft.
description: A beige military attack airplane with twin engines above the fuselage, straight low wings, H-tail, and red accent markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin engines above the fuselage, nose cannon, and wide straight wings with red-tipped edges.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the cockpit canopy, engine nacelles, landing gear, and detailed nose art and insignia.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the back of the engine nacelles.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit, engine placement, landing gear, and additional fuselage markings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with slight anhedral angle and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turbofan engines mounted above the fuselage on pylons.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main units and a single nose gear, all fixed and extended.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single or tandem seat.
